assembly_instructions: Read Gemma 4 as the LIKELY next step beyond Gemma 3: still dense (DeepMind's Gemma identity), but BIGGER, MULTIMODAL ON BOTH IMAGE AND AUDIO, and with an even more aggressive local:global attention plan. Tokens, image patches, AND audio frames enter at the floor. Image goes through SigLIP-2 + Pan&Scan on the left at X=-5; audio goes through a USM-style encoder at X=-7. Their soft tokens are concatenated with text and climb the green residual pillar through 80 dense Transformer blocks. Pattern: 7 LOCAL (sliding-window=512, smaller than Gemma 3's 1024) for every 1 GLOBAL block (full ctx, RoPE base 4M, extends to 1M tokens). The amber column on the left at X=-3.5 is the GQA KV cache — almost entirely from the global layers because local layers only need a 512-token window. The cyan capsule at X=4.5/Y=9.5 is the LATENT-COMPRESSED summary cache — a projected new feature that lets very long contexts share a small persistent latent across requests. The translucent slab at X=8 marks the parallelism + distillation-from-larger-Gemini plan. Click any part for details. THIS IS A FORWARD-LOOKING PROJECTION — numbers should be read as plausible extrapolation, not fact.

info.english_name: Gemma 4 (projected flagship, ~70B dense, native audio + vision)
info.summary: PROJECTED next-generation flagship from Google DeepMind, extrapolated from Gemma 3's published research direction. No public paper at scene time. Best guess: stays DENSE (DeepMind's Gemma identity) at ~70B params (up from 27B), 80 layers × ~7168 hidden, GQA(48/8), local:global attention pushed from 5:1 to 7:1 with a smaller 512-token local window, dual pre+post RMSNorm and QK-Norm preserved, native multimodal on BOTH image (SigLIP-2 + Pan&Scan) AND audio (USM-style encoder), 1M context via global RoPE base 4M, distilled from a larger Gemini teacher.
info.description:
Gemma 4 is presented here as a research-trajectory PROJECTION, not a paper. Where Gemma 3 went dense at 27B with 5:1 LOCAL:GLOBAL attention and image-only multimodal, Gemma 4 likely (i) jumps to ~70B dense (still no MoE — DeepMind has stayed dense across the Gemma line on purpose, citing serving simplicity), (ii) pushes local:global to 7:1 to make 1M context cheap, with a SMALLER 512-token window (was 1024) compensated by global layers carrying more of the long-range work, (iii) adds native AUDIO input via a USM-style encoder, complementing the existing SigLIP image path, (iv) extends global RoPE base from 1M to 4M for clean 1M-token extrapolation, (v) adds an OPTIONAL latent-compressed summary cache that persists across requests for shared system prompts and long documents, (vi) keeps the dual pre+post RMSNorm and QK-Norm of Gemma 3 — both shipped because they're clean stability wins.

Design rationale, in one line each:
• Stay dense at 70B: avoids the operational cost of expert all-to-all; one model, one inference path.
• 7:1 local-global with 512-token window: KV cache is dominated by the 11–12 global layers; locals are nearly free at 1M ctx.
• Audio + image multimodal: audio is the next obvious modality after vision; consistent with Gemini's multimodal-from-day-one philosophy.
• Latent summary cache: shared system prompts get a tiny persistent representation rather than KV reuse — works across model versions.
• Distillation from Gemini: Gemma's whole story; bigger teacher → bigger Gemma student.

facts.label: QK norm
facts.value: RMSNorm on Q & K (kept from Gemma 3)

facts.label: norm placement
facts.value: Pre + post RMSNorm on each sublayer (kept)

facts.label: summary cache
facts.value: Latent persistent cache across requests (projected new)

facts.label: precision
facts.value: BF16 weights, FP8 GEMMs, INT4 KV (projected)

comparisons.delta: Gemma 3 was dense 27B with 5:1 local:global attention, 1024-token local window, image-only multimodal, 128K context, RoPE base 1M for global. Projected Gemma 4 grows to 70B dense, pushes local:global to 7:1 with a HALVED 512-token window, adds audio modality, jumps context 128K → 1M with global RoPE base 4M, and adds a projected new latent-summary cache for prompt sharing across requests. QK-Norm and dual pre+post RMSNorm stay.

Two generations of distance. Gemma 4 keeps the dense + distillation philosophy but replaces every load-bearing block with 7:1 local-global GQA + QK-Norm + dual norm + multimodal vision/audio + 1M context. Vocab 256K stays.

Fundamental philosophical difference: Gemma 4 is DENSE where V4-Pro and GLM-5.1 are sparse MoE. At 70B Gemma 4 activates all 70B per token (vs ~48B for projected GLM-5.1 at 600B total). Gemma's pitch: simpler deployment (no expert all-to-all), smaller absolute footprint, but lower per-parameter capacity ceiling. The 7:1 local-global trick gives Gemma 4 a KV-cache advantage even at 1M context — most layers only need 512-token windows.

Public consensus on GPT-5-class peers is unstable. Gemma 4 stays dense and small relative to closed peers, betting on (i) distillation from a much larger Gemini teacher to close the per-parameter quality gap, (ii) native multimodal audio + image, (iii) the local-global attention plan as the route to cheap 1M context. Where closed peers favour quality at any cost, Gemma 4 favours serving cost.

parts.name: Input Tokens (text)
parts.role: Text input via the multilingual SentencePiece tokeniser (V=256K).

parts.name: Input Image
parts.role: Variable-resolution image input.

parts.name: Input Audio (waveform)
parts.role: PROJECTED new modality: raw 16kHz audio waveform input.

parts.name: Pan-and-Scan Slicer
parts.role: Splits non-square images into multiple 896×896 crops with overlap (kept from Gemma 3).

parts.name: SigLIP-2 (frozen vision encoder)
parts.role: PROJECTED upgrade to SigLIP-2 (improved over SigLIP-1). 256 soft-tokens per crop.

parts.name: USM-style Audio Encoder (frozen)
parts.role: PROJECTED new: 80-mel log-spectrogram → conformer → 30 audio-tokens/sec. Frozen, like SigLIP.
parts.algorithm:
mel = log_mel(audio, n_mels=80)
tokens_audio = conformer(mel)        # 30 tokens / sec
h_audio      = audio_proj(tokens_audio)

parts.name: Vision/Audio → LLM Projector
parts.role: Linear projector mapping vision/audio features into the LLM's d=7168 embedding space.

parts.name: Embedding (V=256K, d=7168)
parts.role: Tied embedding / LM head. ~1.83B params (~3.66 GiB BF16).

parts.name: Residual Stream (d=7168, 80 layers)
parts.role: Pre + post RMSNorm residual carry through 80 layers.

parts.name: × 80 Layers (7 LOCAL : 1 GLOBAL)
parts.role: Marker for the 80-layer depth and 7:1 pattern. ~70 local + ~10 global layers.

parts.name: Block 1 — LOCAL (window=512)
parts.role: Local sliding-window block. Window HALVED from Gemma 3 (1024 → 512) — most long-range work pushed to global layers.

parts.name: Block 2 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 2.

parts.name: Block 3 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 3.

parts.name: Block 4 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 4.

parts.name: Block 5 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 5.

parts.name: Block 6 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 6.

parts.name: Block 7 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 7.

parts.name: Block 8 — GLOBAL (full ctx, RoPE 4M)
parts.role: GLOBAL attention block — attends to the full 1M context. RoPE base 4M (raised from Gemma 3's 1M) keeps angles distinct out to 1M tokens. Dominates attention compute and KV memory at long ctx.

parts.name: Block 9 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 9. Pattern resumes.

parts.name: Block 80 — GLOBAL (final)
parts.role: Final GLOBAL block. (Layers 10–79 elided.)

parts.name: Pre-Attn RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm before attention.

parts.name: GQA · QKV Proj (48 Q / 8 KV)
parts.role: Fused QKV. 48 query heads share 8 KV heads (ratio 6).
parts.algorithm:
Q = x @ W_Q  # 48 heads
K = x @ W_K  # 8 heads
V = x @ W_V  # 8 heads
Q, K = qk_rmsnorm(Q, K)
Q, K = apply_rope(Q, K, base=10K_local OR 4M_global)

parts.name: FlashAttention-3 (local OR global)
parts.role: Same kernel, different mask: window=512 for local, full causal for global.

parts.name: Attention · O-Projection
parts.role: Output projection back to d=7168.

parts.name: Post-Attn RMSNorm
parts.role: POST-norm RMSNorm — Gemma's signature dual-norm placement.

parts.name: GQA KV Cache (mostly global layers)
parts.role: Hetero KV. Local: 512-token ring buffer × 70 layers ≈ very small. Global: 1M ctx × 10 layers — dominates.
parts.memory_footprint: Local ring: ~50 MB. Global: 1M × 8 × 128 × 2 × FP16 × 10 layers ≈ 40 GB / sequence. Projected INT4 KV → ~10 GB.
improvement_ideas: INT4 KV on global layers (projected enabled by default).
Latent summary cache (next part) for shared prompts.

parts.name: Latent Summary Cache (projected new)
parts.role: PROJECTED NEW. A small persistent latent representation of long shared system prompts / documents that survives across requests. Cheaper than KV reuse and works across model versions.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 1 KB per cached prompt-prefix per layer
parts.algorithm:
# offline: encode prompt → tiny latent
latent = compress(prompt_kv)        # ~1 KB / layer
# online: prepend into the global layers' attention
out = attn(Q, concat(latent, K), concat(latent, V))

parts.name: FFN · Pre-RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm before SwiGLU FFN.

parts.name: SwiGLU FFN (d_ff ≈ 28,672, dense)
parts.role: Dense SwiGLU FFN. NO MoE — Gemma's deliberate identity. ~30% bigger d_ff than Gemma 3.
parts.flops_per_token: ≈ 3 × 2·d·d_ff ≈ 1.23 GFLOP / layer (largest GEMM in the block)
parts.memory_footprint: 3 × d × d_ff × BF16 ≈ 1.23 GB / layer; 80 layers ≈ 98 GB total FFN params

parts.name: FFN · Post-RMSNorm
parts.role: Post-norm RMSNorm.

parts.name: LM Head (tied, V=256K)
parts.role: Tied projection back to 256K vocab.

parts.name: LM Loss + Distillation Loss (KL)
parts.role: CE on next-token + KL distillation against a larger Gemini teacher.

parts.name: Teacher (larger Gemini)
parts.role: Frozen larger Gemini whose softmax outputs Gemma 4 distils against — the central piece of Gemma's quality story.

parts.name: AdamW + large batch (TPU)
parts.role: AdamW with very large batch sizes on TPU pods.

parts.name: TPU-pod Parallelism (TP × FSDP × DP)
parts.role: Trained on TPU pods. No EP because the model is dense.

parts.name: ~25 T-token Multimodal Corpus
parts.role: Projected multimodal pre-training corpus: text + image-text + audio-text.
